channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: This sleek Japanese power strip finally fixes your office desk's ugliest pain point
item.description: Kokuyo's Energy Line is offering possibly one of the most unique socket offerings: A plug-in-anywhere power strip that blends into the background.

item.title: Lorde says AI glasses are “not sexy”
item.description: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said on stage.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Wednesday, July 15 (game #1633)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Wednesday, July 15 (game #1130)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Wednesday, July 15 (game #864)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Inside Samsung Display's secret labs, where it builds and stress-tests next-gen displays — and says it's ready for Apple to launch a foldable: 'We mean it, we love other companies to join this market'
item.description: I got an extremely rare inside look at the normally secretive Samsung Display and learned a lot about how they build and test your favorite displays (flexible and otherwise).

item.title: Samsung has a 16TB PCIe 6.0 SSD coming soon with read/write speeds of 28.4GBps and 21.9GBps respectively — but you won't be able to use it anyway
item.description: Samsung's new 16TB SSD reads at 28.4 GB/s, but you can't plug it into a desktop PC just yet.

item.title: Nothing’s good-looking Watch 3 Pro smartwatch is just $69
item.description: While most fitness trackers are losing the screens to keep the price low, the CMF by Nothing Watch 3 Pro is a bit different. The budget-friendly smartwatch with a 1.43-inch OLED display is even cheaper than usual at Amazon, where it costs $69 in every color (the price fluctuates between $79 and $99). Some notable […]

item.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."

item.title: The UK Is Planning a Social Media Curfew for 16- and 17-Year-Olds
item.description: The restrictions, which can be turned off, will include a crackdown on “addictive” app features and will be in addition to a total ban on children under 16 accessing platforms like TikTok and YouTube.

item.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
item.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.
